dependencies.description: NAS must reach TACACS+ server on TCP port 49. Unlike RADIUS (UDP), TACACS+ uses TCP — provides reliable delivery and encrypted full packet body. Firewall must permit TCP/49.
dependencies.name: TCP reachability to TACACS+ server

dependencies.description: IOS-XE: 'aaa new-model' required. NX-OS: 'feature tacacs+' required. IOS-XR: AAA enabled by default.
dependencies.name: AAA model enabled

dependencies.description: NAS and TACACS+ server must have identical shared secret. Entire packet body (except header) is encrypted with this key — mismatch causes complete communication failure.
dependencies.name: Shared secret configured on both sides

dependencies.description: NAS must have IP route to TACACS+ server. VRF-aware configuration needed if TACACS+ server is in management VRF.
dependencies.name: IP routing / reachability

dependencies.description: AAA method lists must reference the TACACS+ server group. Authentication, authorization, and accounting each need their own method lists. ALWAYS include 'local' fallback.
dependencies.name: Method lists mapping to server groups

failure_modes.description: TACACS+ shared key differs between NAS and server — all packets are encrypted with this key, so mismatch causes complete authentication failure

resolutions.action: Re-enter shared key on both NAS and TACACS+ server — type manually
Re-enter shared key on both NAS and TACACS+ server — type manually
Re-enter shared key on both NAS and TACACS+ server — type manually
NX-OS: 'show run | inc tacacs' to see configured key (type-7)
NX-OS: 'show run | inc tacacs' to see configured key (type-7)
NX-OS: 'show run | inc tacacs' to see configured key (type-7)
IOS-XE: check 'show run | sec tacacs' for per-server and global keys
IOS-XE: check 'show run | sec tacacs' for per-server and global keys
IOS-XE: check 'show run | sec tacacs' for per-server and global keys
On ISE: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices > TACACS settings
On ISE: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices > TACACS settings
On ISE: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices > TACACS settings
Test with 'test aaa group tacacs+ <user> <pass> new-code' (IOS-XE)
Test with 'test aaa group tacacs+ <user> <pass> new-code' (IOS-XE)
Test with 'test aaa group tacacs+ <user> <pass> new-code' (IOS-XE)
Temporarily set a simple key (no special chars) on both sides to isolate
Temporarily set a simple key (no special chars) on both sides to isolate
Temporarily set a simple key (no special chars) on both sides to isolate

root_causes.cause: Shared key misconfigured on NAS or server
Copy-paste error with trailing whitespace/newline
Key contains special characters (quotes, $, !) interpreted by shell
Key configured per-server differs from global key
ISE TACACS device entry has different key than switch config

symptoms: Login always fails with 'Authentication failed' or 'incorrect password'
debug tacacs shows 'TACACS+: invalid REPLY received' or garbled data
TACACS+ server logs show 'bad shared secret' or decryption error
'show aaa servers' may show transactions attempted but failing

Verify TCP/49 connectivity: on NX-OS 'telnet <ip> 49 vrf <vrf>'

root_causes.cause: TACACS+ server process down
Firewall blocking TCP port 49
Network path failure (routing, interface down)
Source interface on NAS in wrong VRF or not configured
TACACS+ server listening on non-default port
TCP connection limit reached on server

root_causes.cause: TACACS+ command set on server missing the specific command
Command set uses 'deny' by default and command not explicitly permitted
Privilege level mismatch — user assigned priv 1 but command requires priv 15
Command arguments not matching — 'show' permitted but 'show running-config' denied
Authorization method-list missing or pointing to wrong server group
ISE command set regex incorrect — common with sub-commands and arguments

symptoms: User can log in but certain commands return 'Command authorization failed'
User gets 'Authorization failed' after typing a command
Some commands work but others don't — inconsistent access
debug tacacs shows AUTHOR_FAIL for specific cmd/cmd-arg pairs

Shell profile missing 'priv-lvl=15' in custom attributes

NAS 'aaa authorization exec' not configured — server attributes not applied
ISE returning priv-lvl=0 or priv-lvl=1 by default
Enable authentication using different method than login

failure_modes.description: single-connection (persistent TCP session) between NAS and TACACS+ server becomes stale or causes intermittent failures

resolutions.action: Option 1: Disable single-connection — use per-session TCP (more reliable through firewalls)
Option 1: Disable single-connection — use per-session TCP (more reliable through firewalls)
Option 1: Disable single-connection — use per-session TCP (more reliable through firewalls)
Option 2: Increase firewall/NAT idle timeout above TACACS+ idle timeout
Option 2: Increase firewall/NAT idle timeout above TACACS+ idle timeout
Option 2: Increase firewall/NAT idle timeout above TACACS+ idle timeout
Option 3: Enable TCP keepalives on NAS: 'ip tacacs-server directed-request' (IOS-XE varies)
Option 3: Enable TCP keepalives on NAS: 'ip tacacs-server directed-request' (IOS-XE varies)
Option 3: Enable TCP keepalives on NAS: 'ip tacacs-server directed-request' (IOS-XE varies)
Check ISE TACACS settings: single-connect timeout
Check ISE TACACS settings: single-connect timeout
Check ISE TACACS settings: single-connect timeout
NX-OS: remove 'single-connection' from 'tacacs-server host' line
NX-OS: remove 'single-connection' from 'tacacs-server host' line
NX-OS: remove 'single-connection' from 'tacacs-server host' line

root_causes.cause: Firewall/NAT idle timeout shorter than single-connection idle time
TACACS+ server drops idle connections (ISE default: 120s)
Network device between NAS and server doing stateful inspection with aggressive timeouts
TCP keepalives not configured or not reaching through firewalls
Server-side connection limit reached, refusing new persistent connections

symptoms: Intermittent authentication failures — works sometimes, fails other times
First login after idle period fails, subsequent logins work
debug tacacs shows TCP connection reset or unexpected close
Firewall/NAT timeout killing idle persistent TCP connection

root_causes.cause: Method list missing 'local' fallback: 'aaa authentication login default group tacacs+' (no 'local')
Local user account not configured as fallback
Local user password expired or account disabled
Console line not configured with fallback method list
'aaa authentication login console' overriding default method list without local

failure_modes.scenario: Local fallback not working when all servers down
symptoms: Cannot log in to device at all — locked out
Login hangs for a very long time then gets 'Authentication failed'
Console and VTY access both failing
Only recoverable via password-recovery procedure

messages.description: NAS initiates authentication — sends action type (LOGIN, CHPASS, SENDAUTH), authen_type (ASCII, PAP, CHAP, MSCHAP), and optional username
key_fields: user (username if known at START time)
messages.name: Authentication START
messages.when_sent: User connects to NAS (SSH, console, vty login)

messages.description: Server response to START or CONTINUE — indicates result or requests more data
key_fields: status (PASS, FAIL, GETDATA, GETUSER, GETPASS, RESTART, ERROR)
messages.name: Authentication REPLY
messages.when_sent: After processing authentication START or CONTINUE

messages.description: NAS sends additional data requested by server (username, password, response to challenge)
messages.name: Authentication CONTINUE
messages.when_sent: After server replies with GETUSER, GETPASS, or GETDATA

messages.description: NAS asks server whether a specific action is permitted — includes service type, command, and arguments
messages.name: Authorization REQUEST
messages.when_sent: On every command execution (if command authorization enabled), on shell access (exec authorization), or on service request

messages.description: Server response to authorization request — permits, denies, or modifies the request
key_fields: arg (attribute-value pairs: priv-lvl, acl, autocmd, etc.)
server_msg (text displayed to user on FAIL)
messages.name: Authorization RESPONSE

messages.description: NAS sends accounting record — start, stop, watchdog (interim), or command accounting
messages.name: Accounting REQUEST
messages.when_sent: Session start/stop, command execution (if command accounting enabled), or periodic watchdog

purpose: TCP-based AAA protocol for device administration — separates authentication, authorization, and accounting into independent protocol exchanges with full packet encryption

states.description: No active TACACS+ transaction — NAS ready to initiate new authentication/authorization/accounting exchange
states.name: IDLE

states.description: NAS establishing TCP connection to TACACS+ server (port 49). With single-connection mode, may reuse existing TCP session.
states.name: TCP_CONNECTING

states.description: Authentication START packet sent — waiting for REPLY from TACACS+ server with PASS, FAIL, GETUSER, GETPASS, or ERROR
states.name: AUTHEN_START_SENT

states.description: Multi-round authentication in progress — NAS sending CONTINUE packets with user-supplied data (username, password)
states.name: AUTHEN_CONTINUE

states.description: Authentication failed — bad credentials or policy denial
states.name: AUTHEN_FAIL

states.description: Authorization REQUEST sent — NAS asking TACACS+ server whether this user/command is permitted
states.name: AUTHOR_REQUEST_SENT

states.description: Authorization succeeded with PASS_ADD — server returned additional attributes (privilege level, autocommand, etc.)
states.name: AUTHOR_PASS_ADD

states.description: Authorization succeeded with PASS_REPL — server replaced the requested command/args with modified values
states.name: AUTHOR_PASS_REPL

states.description: Authorization denied — user not permitted to execute the requested command or access the requested service
states.name: AUTHOR_FAIL

states.description: Accounting REQUEST sent — NAS reporting session event (start, stop, command) to TACACS+ server
states.name: ACCT_REQUEST_SENT

states.description: TACACS+ server marked dead after TCP connection failures or timeouts — NAS skips this server until deadtime expires
states.name: SERVER_DEAD

timers.description: Time to wait for TCP connection establishment to TACACS+ server
timers.name: tcp-timeout
timers.on_expiry: TCP connection attempt abandoned, try next server

os_defaults.notes: Configured via 'tacacs-server timeout' or per-server 'timeout' under 'tacacs server <name>'
Configured under 'tacacs-server host' or 'aaa group server tacacs+'

timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster failover (2-3s recommended). Default 5s means 5s delay per dead server before trying the next one.

timers.description: Time a TACACS+ server remains marked dead after TCP failures — requests skip dead servers during this period
timers.name: deadtime
timers.on_expiry: Server returned to active pool for probing
timers.tuning_advice: ALWAYS configure deadtime (5-15 min). Without it, every login attempt waits for TCP timeout to dead server. Critical when primary server is down.

timers.description: Maximum time for a complete TACACS+ transaction (connect + exchange + response) before aborting
timers.name: session-timeout
timers.on_expiry: TACACS+ transaction aborted, fallback to next method in method-list

timers.description: In single-connection mode, how long to keep the TCP session open while idle before tearing it down
timers.name: single-connection-idle
timers.on_expiry: Idle TCP connection torn down, next request opens new connection
timers.tuning_advice: Single-connection reduces TCP setup overhead but can cause issues if the persistent TCP session goes stale through firewalls (NAT/PAT timeouts) or if server-side session limit is reached.

transitions.trigger: User login or command execution triggers AAA request
TCP connection established, authentication START sent

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check TCP/49 reachability, firewall rules, TACACS+ server process status
transitions.trigger: TCP connection refused or timed out
Server replies with GETUSER or GETPASS — requesting additional credentials from NAS
Server replies with PASS — authentication successful
Server replies with FAIL — credentials rejected
Server replies with PASS after receiving credentials
Server replies with FAIL — credentials rejected
Authentication succeeded, NAS sends authorization REQUEST for the requested service (shell, command, etc.)
Server responds with PASS_ADD — authorized with additional attributes
Server responds with PASS_REPL — authorized with replaced attributes

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check TACACS+ policy: user may be missing shell profile, command set, or privilege level on the server
transitions.trigger: Server responds with FAIL — command or service denied
Authorized action triggers accounting record (command or session event)
Accounting SUCCESS received or timeout — transaction complete
Authentication failure processed, connection closed or returned to pool
Deadtime expires — server returned to active pool

notes.note: IOS-XE TACACS+: 'aaa new-model' required. Named server syntax: 'tacacs server <name>' / 'address ipv4 <ip>' / 'key <secret>'. Server groups: 'aaa group server tacacs+ <name>'. Test: 'test aaa group tacacs+ <user> <pass> new-code'.

IOS-XE command authorization: 'aaa authorization commands <level> default group tacacs+ local' — authorizes EVERY command at that privilege level. Use with caution — if TACACS+ server is slow or unreachable, every command will hang. ALWAYS include 'local' fallback. Consider 'if-authenticated' keyword as alternative.

IOS-XE privilege levels: 0 (minimal), 1 (user EXEC), 15 (privileged EXEC). TACACS+ returns priv-lvl=<N> in shell profile. Custom levels 2-14 supported via 'privilege exec level <N> <command>'. Different from IOS-XR task-based model.

IOS-XE TACACS+ accounting: for compliance, configure all three: 'aaa accounting exec', 'aaa accounting commands 15', and 'aaa accounting system'. This gives session logs + command audit trail + reload/config events.
IOS-XR TACACS+: configured under 'tacacs-server host <ip> port 49 key <secret>'. Server groups: 'aaa group server tacacs+ <name>'. Source interface via 'source-interface' under group config.

IOS-XR task-based authorization: does NOT use IOS privilege levels. Uses task-groups (read, write, execute, debug per feature). TACACS+ must return task strings, not priv-lvl. Pre-defined groups: root-system, root-lr, cisco-support, operator. Custom task-groups supported. This is the #1 gotcha when migrating from IOS/NX-OS.

IOS-XR NACM (NETCONF Access Control Model): RFC 8341. For NETCONF/ gNMI access, IOS-XR uses NACM rules in addition to or instead of TACACS+ authorization. Task-groups map to NACM groups. Consider this for devices managed via automation/NETCONF.

IOS-XR local user fallback: 'aaa authentication login default group tacacs+ local'. Create local user with 'username <user> group root-system secret <pass>'. CRITICAL for preventing lockout when TACACS+ servers unreachable.

NX-OS TACACS+: requires 'feature tacacs+'. Primary AAA protocol for device administration on Nexus. Configured via 'tacacs-server host <ip> key <secret>'. Server groups via 'aaa group server tacacs+'. Test with 'test aaa server tacacs+ <ip> <user> <pass>'.

NX-OS VRF-aware TACACS+: use 'use-vrf management' (or other VRF) under 'aaa group server tacacs+'. Critical for Nexus where management traffic is in mgmt VRF by default. Without this, TACACS+ traffic goes out default VRF and fails.

NX-OS TACACS+ user roles: supports mapping TACACS+ users to NX-OS roles via cisco-av-pair: 'shell:roles="network-admin"'. Roles: network-admin (full), network-operator (read-only), vdc-admin, vdc-operator, or custom roles.
NX-OS TACACS+ single-connection: supported via 'single-connection' keyword on tacacs-server host. Can cause issues through firewalls — disable if intermittent failures occur.
